senses.definition: A room or hall at the entrance to a chapel, usually opening into the main worship area.
examples: Visitors gathered quietly in the antechapel before the service began.
The college antechapel is lined with memorial plaques.

senses.definition: Plural of antechapel: entrance rooms or halls leading into chapels.
examples: Many historic colleges have large antechapels attached to their main chapels.
The tour included visits to several antechapels and cloisters.

senses.definition: A scientific or taxonomic name, not used in general contemporary English, probably referring to a genus of small mammals.
examples: The term Antechinomys appears only in specialist zoological texts.
Most learners of English will never need to use Antechinomys.

senses.definition: An enclosed or reserved space at the front of the choir area in a church, often for clergy and singers.
examples: The clergy assembled in the antechoir before processing to the altar.
Tourists are not allowed to enter the antechoir during services.

senses.definition: Plural of antechoir: enclosed spaces at the front of the choir area in churches.
examples: The cathedral complex includes several small antechoirs.
Screens separated the antechoirs from the main nave.

senses.definition: A rarely used historical word for a space or building in front of a church.
examples: The old plan mentioned an antechurch that no longer exists.
Only specialist texts refer to the structure as an antechurch.

senses.definition: Relating to the period before the classical age in art, literature, or history.
examples: The course focuses on anteclassical Greek sculpture.
Some scholars specialize in anteclassical Latin literature.

senses.definition: A small room or space adjoining a closet, especially in older buildings.
examples: The antecloset was used for storing cleaning tools.
In the old house, an antecloset separated the bedroom from the main closet.

senses.definition: Located in front of the colon, especially describing the position of part of the intestine or a surgical connection.
examples: The surgeon chose an antecolic route for the gastric bypass.
Antecolic placement of the loop can reduce complications.

senses.definition: In some Christian churches, the part of the Communion service that comes before the consecration of the bread and wine.
examples: On certain Sundays the service stops after Antecommunion.
The prayers of Antecommunion were said in traditional language.

senses.definition: Occurring before a consonant, especially referring to a sound or letter in linguistics.
examples: The vowel has a different quality in anteconsonantal position.
This change happens only in anteconsonantal environments.

senses.definition: The front part or anterior horn of a lateral ventricle in the brain.
examples: Lesions were noted near the antecornu of the lateral ventricle.
The MRI showed changes around the antecornu region.

senses.definition: An outer or front court in front of a main courtyard or building.
examples: Visitors entered through the antecourt before reaching the palace.
The antecourt was decorated with fountains and statues.

senses.definition: Situated in front of the coxa, especially in the body of an insect or other arthropod.
examples: The antecoxal region is marked by a distinct groove.
Antecoxal sclerites are useful in identifying the species.

senses.definition: Relating to the inner or front part of the elbow.
examples: Blood was drawn from the antecubital vein.
Place the cuff around the upper arm above the antecubital area.

senses.definition: A forward bend or curve in an organ or structure, especially in anatomy.
examples: Antecurvature of the spine was visible on the scan.
The antecurvature of the vessel affected blood flow.

senses.definition: Paid the amount that each player must put into the pot at the start of a round of cards.
examples: Everyone anted before the dealer gave out the cards.
He anted and waited for the next hand.

senses.definition: To be or happen earlier in time than something else.
examples: These ruins antedate the nearby temple by two centuries.
The legal system antedates the current constitution.

senses.definition: To put a date on a document that is earlier than the real date.
examples: The contract was antedated to the first of January.
He antedated the letter for accounting reasons.

senses.definition: Past tense of antedate: happened earlier in time than something else.
examples: The chapel clearly antedated the surrounding buildings.
This tradition antedated written records.

senses.definition: Past tense of antedate: had an earlier date written on it than the real date.
examples: The agreement was antedated to avoid the new tax rules.
He signed and antedated the cheque.

senses.definition: Third person singular of antedate: happens or exists earlier in time than something else.
examples: This custom antedates the arrival of Europeans.
The stone bridge antedates the modern road.

senses.definition: Third person singular of antedate: has a date written that is earlier than the real date.
examples: The lease antedates the new regulations.
Her letter antedates the official announcement.

senses.definition: Present participle of antedate: happening earlier in time than something else.
examples: Findings of artefacts antedating the Roman period changed the history books.
Documents antedating the war are kept in the archive.

senses.definition: Present participle of antedate: putting an earlier date on a document than the real date.
examples: Antedating the contract could be considered fraud.
They were accused of antedating several invoices.

senses.definition: Happening or existing just before dawn.
examples: They began their climb in the antedawn darkness.
An antedawn mist hung over the fields.

senses.definition: Relating to the time before a great flood, especially the flood described in the Bible.
examples: The tale describes antediluvial civilizations lost beneath the sea.
Some legends speak of antediluvial kings and heroes.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to the period before a great flood, especially the Biblical flood, or in an extremely old-fashioned way.
examples: The customs described seem almost antediluvially ancient.
He lived antediluvially, without modern devices or comforts.

senses.definition: Relating to the time before the Biblical Flood in the story of Noah.
examples: Scholars debated the meaning of antediluvian fossils.
The book describes antediluvian landscapes and creatures.

senses.definition: Extremely old or old-fashioned.
examples: His views on education are positively antediluvian.
They replaced the antediluvian heating system at last.

senses.definition: A person or thing that is very old or old-fashioned, sometimes humorously.
examples: In their eyes he was an antediluvian who refused to use email.
The law is an antediluvian that no one bothers to change.

senses.definition: People or things that are very old or extremely old-fashioned, sometimes used humorously.
examples: The new recruits joked that the managers were antediluvians.
He dismissed the critics as antediluvians stuck in the past.

senses.definition: A scientific or taxonomic name, especially for a genus of marine animals, not used in general contemporary English.
examples: Antedon appears frequently in zoological classification charts.
Most non-specialists have never heard of Antedon.

senses.definition: A technical or chemical term appearing only in specialist scientific usage, with no common meaning in general English.
examples: The compound antedonin is mentioned only in older research papers.
Students are unlikely to encounter the term antedonin outside of specialized texts.

senses.definition: Situated in front of the dorsal (back) part of a body or structure.
examples: The ante­dorsal scales help distinguish this species of fish.
An ante­dorsal marking appears just before the dorsal fin.

senses.definition: Simple past of ante: paid the required stake at the beginning of a card game.
examples: They all anteed before the first hand was dealt.
She anteed and waited for the dealer to shuffle.

senses.definition: A rare or nonstandard variant of artefact, meaning an object made or shaped by humans, especially one of historical interest.
examples: The stone tools were described as antefacts of early hunters.
Only a few texts use the spelling antefact instead of artefact.

senses.definition: Occurring in the period just before a fever or febrile illness.
examples: Patients may feel tired in the antefebrile stage.
An antefebrile rash can appear before the temperature rises.

senses.definition: An upright ornament along the edge of a tiled roof that hides the ends of the tiles.
examples: The temple roof was decorated with terracotta antefixes.
Each antefix showed the face of a mythical creature.

senses.definition: Plural form of antefix, especially used in archaeological or art-historical writing.
examples: The museum displays several Etruscan antefixa.
Roman antefixa often show floral designs.

senses.definition: Another Latin-style plural form of antefix used in specialized texts.
examples: The catalogue lists all surviving antefixae from the site.
Fragments of painted antefixae were found in the ruins.

senses.definition: Relating to or shaped like an antefix on a roof.
examples: The antefixal decoration runs along the front of the temple.
Antefixal motifs were common in ancient Greek architecture.

senses.definition: Plural of antefix: upright ornaments placed along the eaves of a tiled roof to hide the tile ends.
examples: The line of antefixes gave the roof a rhythmic pattern.
Many antefixes have been lost, but a few remain in place.

senses.definition: Bent or curved forward, especially describing an organ in anatomy.
examples: The uterus was anteflected and pressing on the bladder.
Anteflected segments of the spine were noted on the X-ray.

senses.definition: Bent forward at an angle, especially referring to a body organ such as the uterus.
examples: An anteflexed uterus is considered a normal variation.
The anteflexed position may cause some discomfort.

senses.definition: A forward bend or curvature, especially of an organ such as the uterus.
examples: Uterine anteflexion is common and usually harmless.
Pain may be associated with extreme anteflexion of the spine.

senses.definition: A forked internal structure in the thorax of certain insects, located toward the front.
examples: The antefurca serves as an attachment point for muscles.
Species can be distinguished by the shape of the antefurca.

senses.definition: Plural of antefurca: the front forked internal thoracic structures in certain insects.
examples: The antefurcae were compared across different beetle species.
In this group, the antefurcae are strongly sclerotized.

senses.definition: Relating to or situated near the antefurca in an insect’s thorax.
examples: The antefurcal area is marked by a distinct ridge.
Antefurcal muscles attach along this internal brace.

senses.definition: A very rare traditional grammar term for a verb tense that expresses an action that will be completed before a specific future time.
examples: Older grammars sometimes refer to the future perfect as the antefuture.
The term antefuture is not used in modern teaching materials.

senses.definition: A garden or yard in front of a house or building.
examples: The antegarden was filled with roses and small trees.
Guests walked through the antegarden to reach the front door.

senses.definition: Moving or directed forward in the normal direction, especially of flow in the body.
examples: The procedure restores antegrade blood flow to the limb.
Antegrade movement of the contrast dye was observed.

senses.definition: In a forward or normal direction of movement or flow.
examples: The stent was advanced antegrade through the artery.
Fluid travels antegrade along the intestinal tract.

senses.definition: A small hall or room leading into a larger hall.
examples: Guests gathered in the antehall before entering the ballroom.
Coats were left in the antehall near the entrance.

senses.definition: Relating to the time before recorded history.
examples: They studied antehistoric human settlement in the region.
Antehistoric art is difficult to interpret.

senses.definition: Existing or occurring before the appearance of humans.
examples: The film imagines antehuman landscapes filled with giant reptiles.
Geologists study antehuman climate patterns.

senses.definition: The front part of the pituitary gland that produces several important hormones.
examples: Damage to the antehypophysis can disrupt growth and metabolism.
Hormones from the antehypophysis affect many body functions.
